tasks.title: Workspace seed expansion — publish all legacy tab dashboards
tasks.description: Expand lib/workspace/seed.ts to include published WorkspaceDashboard entries for every tab currently in equipment.tabs (lib/data.ts) that is not yet covered by an existing published seed entry. This ensures Equipment Home has visual parity after removing dashboardCards as the data source.
tasks.details:
Add 7 new published dashboard skeletons to the dashSkels array in buildWorkspaceSeed():
- Coke Drum (cokerId): 'Demo Engineer Team's Dashboard' (widgetsKey: "Demo Engineer Team's Dashboard", key: 'legacy-coker-demo'), 'Process' (widgetsKey: 'Process', key: 'legacy-coker-proc'), 'Fatigue' (widgetsKey: 'Fatigue', key: 'legacy-coker-fat'), 'Bulging' (widgetsKey: 'Bulging', key: 'legacy-coker-bul') — all status: 'published', ownerUserId: 'user-nhan', folder: null
- HCU (hcuId): 'Reactor Health' (widgetsKey: 'Reactor Health', key: 'legacy-hcu-rea'), 'Process Control' (widgetsKey: 'Process Control', key: 'legacy-hcu-proc'), 'Maintenance' (widgetsKey: 'Maintenance', key: 'legacy-hcu-maint') — all status: 'published', ownerUserId: 'user-ben', folder: null
All 7 new entries must use stable deterministic IDs via the existing id = `dash-${d.key}` pattern (e.g., 'dash-legacy-coker-demo').
Keep all existing seed entries unchanged. Ensure DEFAULT_GRIDS lookups succeed for all new widgetsKey values (they already exist in layouts.ts).
tasks.testStrategy: After change: verify buildWorkspaceSeed().dashboards has 20 entries (13 existing + 7 new); all 7 new entries have lifecycleStatus='published' and deletedAt=null; widgetsKey resolves to non-empty widgets array; IDs are stable on repeated calls.

tasks.title: Create lib/workspace-data.ts — canonical bridge adapter
tasks.description: Create lib/workspace-data.ts providing the EquipmentHomeDashCard adapter type and all bridge utility functions used by Equipment Home, Home Page, Site/Plant Overview, and WhatIf to derive dashboard data from the Workspace store.
tasks.details:
Export the following from lib/workspace-data.ts:

1. `EquipmentHomeDashCard` interface:
   { id: string; equipment: string; equipId: string; tag: string; metrics: { value1: string; value2: string } }
   This is a drop-in structural replacement for the legacy `dashboardCards[0]` shape.

2. `getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment(equipmentId: string, dashboards: WorkspaceDashboard[]): EquipmentHomeDashCard[]`
   - Filter by equipmentId, lifecycleStatus === 'published', deletedAt === null
   - Map each WorkspaceDashboard: id = d.id, equipId = d.equipmentId, equipment = look up from sites (equipment.name), tag = d.name
   - metrics: deterministic mock derived from a djb2-style hash of d.id ('XX%' for value1, '0.00X%' for value2)
   - Return sorted by d.publishedAt desc (most recent first)

3. `getDashboardById(id: string, dashboards: WorkspaceDashboard[]): WorkspaceDashboard | undefined`
   - Simple find by id

4. `LEGACY_TAG_TO_WORKSPACE_ID` — a const Record<string, string> mapping 'equipmentId:legacyTag' to the corresponding stable WorkspaceDashboard.id from the seed:
   e.g., 'equipment-a:Demo Engineer Team\'s Dashboard' -> 'dash-legacy-coker-demo'
        'equipment-a:Monitoring' -> 'dash-nhan-coker-mon'
        'equipment-a:Process' -> 'dash-legacy-coker-proc'
        'equipment-a:Fatigue' -> 'dash-legacy-coker-fat'
        'equipment-a:Bulging' -> 'dash-legacy-coker-bul'
        'equipment-a:Cracking' -> 'dash-nhan-coker-cra'
        'equipment-b:Overview' -> 'dash-ben-hcu-over'
        'equipment-b:Reactor Health' -> 'dash-legacy-hcu-rea'
        'equipment-b:Process Control' -> 'dash-legacy-hcu-proc'
        'equipment-b:Maintenance' -> 'dash-legacy-hcu-maint'
        'equipment-c:SMR Pigtail Integrity' -> 'dash-nhan-smr-pig'

5. `getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag(equipmentId: string, tag: string, dashboards: WorkspaceDashboard[]): string | undefined`
   - Look up LEGACY_TAG_TO_WORKSPACE_ID with key `${equipmentId}:${tag}`
   - If not found, fall back to dashboards.find(d => d.equipmentId === equipmentId && d.name === tag && d.lifecycleStatus === 'published')?.id
   - Final fallback: dashboards.find(d => d.equipmentId === equipmentId && d.lifecycleStatus === 'published' && !d.deletedAt)?.id

Import WorkspaceDashboard from lib/workspace/types; import sites from lib/data for equipment name resolution. No React imports (pure utility).
tasks.testStrategy: Spot check: getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment('equipment-a', WORKSPACE_SEED.dashboards) returns 6 cards with equipId='equipment-a'; each has non-empty tag (= dashboard name), non-empty metrics. getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag('equipment-a', 'Monitoring', ...) returns 'dash-nhan-coker-mon'. getDashboardById('dash-legacy-coker-demo', ...) returns the demo dashboard.

tasks.title: Wire Equipment Home to Workspace store (DashboardSection + DashboardPopup + cross-tab)
tasks.description: Replace dashboardCards-based rendering in components/views/equipment-home.tsx with Workspace store data. DashboardSection reads published WorkspaceDashboard records; DashboardPopup uses WorkspaceDashboard.widgets for the grid while preserving all WIF overlay behavior intact.
tasks.details:
Changes in components/views/equipment-home.tsx:

1. IMPORTS: Remove imports of `dashboardCards`, `DEFAULT_WIDGET_SETS`, `buildDefaultGrid`. Add imports: `useWorkspaceStore` from lib/workspace/store; `getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment`, `getDashboardById`, `EquipmentHomeDashCard` from lib/workspace-data.

2. DashboardSection:
   - Remove `const equipCards = dashboardCards.filter(c => c.equipId === equipment.id)`
   - Subscribe to Workspace store: `const rawDashboards = useWorkspaceStore(useShallow(s => s.dashboards))`
   - Derive `const equipCards = useMemo(() => getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment(equipment.id, rawDashboards), [equipment.id, rawDashboards])`
   - `handleCardClick(card.id, card.id)` — pass workspace dashboard ID as both cardId and the open-popup identifier (not cardTag)

3. DashboardPopup:
   - Rename prop `cardTag: string` → `dashboardId: string`
   - Subscribe: `const rawDashboards = useWorkspaceStore(useShallow(s => s.dashboards))`
   - `const dashboard = useMemo(() => getDashboardById(dashboardId, rawDashboards), [dashboardId, rawDashboards])`
   - Replace widget grid source: remove `const widgetSet = DEFAULT_WIDGET_SETS[cardTag] ?? []` and `const grid = buildDefaultGrid(widgetSet)`; use `const grid = dashboard?.widgets ?? []`
   - Grid rendering: `grid.map(gw => ...)` is unchanged; layout items `layouts = grid.map(gw => gw.layout)` is unchanged
   - Display name: replace `equipment.name — {cardTag}` with `equipment.name — {dashboard?.name ?? dashboardId}`
   - New-tab URL: change from `/equipment-dashboard/${encodeURIComponent(equipment.id)}/${encodeURIComponent(cardTag)}/full` to `/dashboards/${encodeURIComponent(dashboardId)}/full`
   - Report name: replace cardTag in filename with `dashboard?.name ?? dashboardId`
   - aria-label: use `dashboard?.name ?? dashboardId`
   - KEEP ALL UNCHANGED: viewedDataIds state, setViewedDataIds, visualReportGenerated, shareOpen, scenarioForEquipment, equipmentRuns, WIF overlay panel (viewedDataIds checkbox section), WidgetViewResolver props (viewType, equipmentId, viewedDataIds, scenarioRuns) — these must not change

4. OpenElsewhereBadge:
   - Change props from `{ equipmentId, tag }` to `{ dashboardId: string }`
   - Remove `getEquipmentDashboardPresenceKey` helper function entirely
   - Call `useDashboardOpenElsewhereCount(dashboardId)` directly
   - Update all usages inside DashboardSection cards and DashboardPopup header to pass `dashboardId` (workspace ID)

5. EquipmentHomeView:
   - State: rename `activePopupTag` → `activePopupDashboardId` (string | null)
   - `openPopup` callback: receives `dashboardId: string`
   - Auto-open useEffect: change from `setActivePopupTag(equipmentHomeAutoOpenTab)` to `setActivePopupDashboardId(equipmentHomeAutoOpenTab)` (since it now carries a workspace ID)
   - DashboardPopup JSX: `dashboardId={activePopupDashboardId!}`

6. recents: `handleCardClick` calls `addRecentDashboard(card.id)` where card.id is WorkspaceDashboard.id (no semantic change, just correct ID)

Do NOT change: WIF overlay logic, scenarioRuns derivation, viewedDataIds behavior, WidgetViewResolver rendering, visual layout, favorites display.
tasks.testStrategy: Equipment Home renders same number of dashboard cards as before (6 for Coke Drum, 4 for HCU, 1 for SMR). Popup opens and shows correct dashboard name. Widget grid renders (uses dashboard.widgets). WIF overlay panel (Viewed Data section) still shows live + run checkboxes. Open-in-new-tab opens /dashboards/[uuid]/full. No TypeScript errors.

tasks.title: Wire Home Page recents/favorites to Workspace store
tasks.description: Update components/views/home-view.tsx so that DashboardCardRow, RecentDashboardsModule, FavoriteDashboardsModule, and navigateToDashboardFromCard resolve dashboard display data from the Workspace store instead of the static dashboardCards array.
tasks.details:
Changes in components/views/home-view.tsx:

1. IMPORTS: Remove `dashboardCards` import from lib/data. Add `useWorkspaceStore` from lib/workspace/store; add `getDashboardById`, `EquipmentHomeDashCard`, `getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment` from lib/workspace-data.

2. DashboardCardRow component:
   - Add `const rawDashboards = useWorkspaceStore(useShallow(s => s.dashboards))`
   - Replace `const cards = cardIds.map(id => dashboardCards.find(c => c.id === id)).filter(Boolean)` with:
     `const cards = useMemo(() => { const allPublished = rawDashboards.filter(d => d.lifecycleStatus === 'published' && !d.deletedAt); return cardIds.map(id => { const ws = allPublished.find(d => d.id === id); if (!ws) return null; return getDashboardById_asCard(ws) }).filter(Boolean) }, [cardIds, rawDashboards])`
   - Add helper `getDashboardById_asCard(ws: WorkspaceDashboard): EquipmentHomeDashCard` inline or as a local function that maps WorkspaceDashboard to the EquipmentHomeDashCard shape
   - For thumbnailSrc: use `ws.thumbnailUrl ?? getEquipmentDashboardThumbnail(ws.equipmentId)` (thumbnailUrl from workspace store takes precedence)
   - Typing: update all `(typeof dashboardCards)[0]` type references to `EquipmentHomeDashCard`

3. navigateToDashboardFromCard:
   - Signature change: `card: EquipmentHomeDashCard` instead of `(typeof dashboardCards)[0]`
   - Logic unchanged: `addRecentDashboard(card.id)`, find site/plant by `card.equipId`, setCurrentPath with equipment: card.equipId and tab: card.tag
   - tab in setCurrentPath is now `card.tag` which equals WorkspaceDashboard.name — keep this as-is (used for path context, Equipment Home will resolve the actual popup via getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag in Task 3's auto-open effect)

4. Global search 'dashboard' result handler (already in home-view.tsx near line 105):
   - `const card = dashboardCards.find(c => c.equipId === result.equipmentId && c.tag === result.tab)` → replace with Workspace store lookup:
     `const wsId = getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag(result.equipmentId, result.tab, useWorkspaceStore.getState().dashboards)`
   - `if (wsId) addRecentDashboard(wsId)` (no longer look for card)
   - Navigation path: `tab: result.tab` unchanged (Equipment Home resolves it)

Do NOT change: visual rendering of DashboardCard component, empty state messages, RecentDashboardsModule/FavoriteDashboardsModule JSX structure, favorites toggle logic.
tasks.testStrategy: Home page renders. Recent dashboards section shows empty state (no items yet) or populated items when recents have been added via Equipment Home. Clicking a recent card navigates to Equipment Home. No TypeScript errors. No dashboardCards import remains.

tasks.title: Wire Site/Plant Overview tab stacks to Workspace store
tasks.description: Update components/views/site-overview.tsx and components/views/plant-overview.tsx so their DashboardTabStack card lists are derived from published WorkspaceDashboard records instead of the static dashboardCards array.
tasks.details:
Changes in components/views/site-overview.tsx:
1. IMPORTS: Remove `dashboardCards` import. Add `useWorkspaceStore` from lib/workspace/store; add `getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment`, `EquipmentHomeDashCard` from lib/workspace-data; add `useShallow` from zustand/react/shallow; add `useMemo` from react.
2. Subscribe: `const rawDashboards = useWorkspaceStore(useShallow(s => s.dashboards))`
3. Replace: `const siteCards = dashboardCards.filter(c => site.plants.some(p => p.equipment.some(eq => eq.id === c.equipId)))` with:
   `const siteCards = useMemo(() => { const equipIds = new Set(site.plants.flatMap(p => p.equipment.map(e => e.id))); return rawDashboards.filter(d => d.lifecycleStatus === 'published' && !d.deletedAt && equipIds.has(d.equipmentId)).map(d => getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment(d.equipmentId, rawDashboards).find(c => c.id === d.id)).filter(Boolean) as EquipmentHomeDashCard[] }, [site, rawDashboards])`
   OR simpler: `const siteCards = useMemo(() => { const result: EquipmentHomeDashCard[] = []; for (const plant of site.plants) for (const eq of plant.equipment) result.push(...getPublishedDashboardsForEquipment(eq.id, rawDashboards)); return result; }, [site, rawDashboards])`
4. handleDashboardClick: `addRecentDashboard(card.id)` and `setEquipmentHomeAutoOpenTab(card.id)` where card.id is now WorkspaceDashboard.id
5. groupedCards derivation uses card.equipId for grouping — unchanged

Same pattern for components/views/plant-overview.tsx:
- Replace `const plantCards = dashboardCards.filter(...)` with Workspace store derivation for the plant's equipment
- handleDashboardClick: same WorkspaceDashboard.id pattern

Both files: remove dashboardCards import, add workspace-data imports.
tasks.testStrategy: Site Overview and Plant Overview render their dashboard tab stacks with the same number of cards as before. Clicking a card navigates to Equipment Home and auto-opens the correct popup. addRecentDashboard receives a workspace ID. No TypeScript errors.

tasks.title: Wire WhatIf Compare Data to Workspace IDs + final cleanup
tasks.description: Update the What-If tool's Compare Data navigation to resolve a WorkspaceDashboard.id as the auto-open target instead of a legacy tab name string. Mark dashboardCards as deprecated. Remove all remaining legacy tab-name-based identifier usage. Final typecheck pass.
tasks.details:
1. components/views/whatif-tool-view.tsx — onCompareData handler:
   - Import `useWorkspaceStore` from lib/workspace/store
   - Import `getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag` from lib/workspace-data
   - Inside the handler: `const { site, plant, tab } = findAssetPathForEquipment(panel.session.equipmentId)` — keep this for site/plant path context
   - After getting tab, add: `const wsDashboards = useWorkspaceStore.getState().dashboards`
     `const targetDashboardId = getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag(panel.session.equipmentId, panel.session.selectedDashboards?.[0] ?? tab, wsDashboards) ?? tab`
   - Call `setEquipmentHomeAutoOpenTab(targetDashboardId)` (was `setEquipmentHomeAutoOpenTab(tab)`)
   - All other lines in onCompareData unchanged (setCurrentPath, setWhatIfDashboardAutoSelectRunId, setCurrentView, setViewMode)

   Note: panel.session.selectedDashboards?.[0] is the first dashboard the WIF session was run for (e.g. 'Overview'); getWorkspaceDashboardIdForTag maps it to a WorkspaceDashboard.id. If the equipment-a Coke Drum WIF has selectedDashboards = ['Demo Engineer Team\'s Dashboard'], the result is 'dash-legacy-coker-demo'.

2. lib/data.ts:
   - Add `@deprecated Use useWorkspaceStore().dashboards (lib/workspace/store) via lib/workspace-data.ts adapters instead.` JSDoc comment above the dashboardCards export

3. Verify no remaining non-deprecated import of dashboardCards in equipment-home.tsx, home-view.tsx, site-overview.tsx, plant-overview.tsx after Tasks 3, 4, 5 are complete.

4. lib/store.ts: no change needed — recentDashboardIds and favoriteDashboardIds are opaque string arrays; they now hold WorkspaceDashboard.id values which is correct.

5. Run TypeScript type check: `npx tsc --noEmit` and resolve any type errors introduced by the migration (likely from card shape changes in home-view.tsx and site-overview.tsx).
tasks.testStrategy: 1. WhatIf tool: clicking Compare Data on a completed run navigates to Equipment Home and auto-opens a popup (not a blank page). 2. The auto-opened popup shows a valid dashboard from the Workspace store. 3. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with no errors. 4. No console errors on Equipment Home, Home Page, Site Overview, or Plant Overview.
